results.notes: Celery workers either rename argv[0] to 'celery' or launch as python /path/to/celery.
Ceph daemons are distinct binaries — match by name.
Test fixture is a python mock (`python server.py run -h 0.0.0.0`). Real Citrix Hypervisor would need a different rule against the actual XenServer toolstack.
comm is 15-char-truncated from 'clickhouse-server', which is unique enough on its own.
Confluent Platform: brokers (kafka.Kafka), zookeeper, schema registry, ksql, control center, kafka-rest.
Consul agent — name + explicit subcommand to avoid matching other consul invocations.
Couchbase erlang VM. Targets all 3 beam.smp roles (babysitter, ns_server, ns_couchdb) that share the /opt/couchbase prefix in argv[0]; a tighter rule could pick a single role.

results.notes: Test fixture runs OpenSearch; production may run Elasticsearch — accept either main class.
Test fixture uses VMware's vcsim simulator. Real ESXi would be queried over the API, not by host process.
go_expvar targets any Go binary publishing expvars; test fixture binary is named test_expvar.
Hudi test fixture runs as Spark — same predicate as the spark integration.

results.notes: Same target as kafka_consumer.
Matches NameNode, DataNode, ResourceManager, NodeManager, JobHistoryServer, etc.
Test fixture launches marathon as 'java -jar marathon-assembly-1.3.0.jar' rather than via the documented mesosphere.marathon.Main class.
node /usr/local/bin/n8n — comm is unreliable (shows up as 'MainThread' here).

nfsstat is a check against /proc/net/rpc, not a daemon. The test fixture provides rpcbind; this rule matches what's present, though the production integration probably doesn't rely on process discovery.

nginx rewrites argv[0] to include spaces ('nginx: master process nginx -g daemon off;'). The space-split args this script derives doesn't preserve that single argv element, so use cmdline.contains for the role marker.

survivor_detail.name: nginx
survivor_detail.cmdline: nginx: master process nginx -g daemon off;

results.notes: PostgreSQL master is launched with -D <datadir> or -c config_file=. Background workers (checkpointer, walwriter, etc.) rewrite argv[0] to 'postgres: <role>' and don't carry these flags.
'prefect server start' and 'prefect worker start' — target both.
rabbitmq runs as Erlang beam.smp. Distinguish from couchbase/riak beam.smp via the rabbit-specific path/cookie in args.

results.notes: Match the native raylet and gcs_server binaries explicitly. The python client.server is launched as 'python -m ray.util.client.server ...', so check args without falling back to a 'raylet' substring (which would also match worker processes that reference the raylet socket path).

results.notes: SonarQube launches sonar-application (the wrapper) plus separate JVMs for WebServer and CeServer; elasticsearch in the same container is matched by the elastic rule but distinguished here by not appearing in this list.
Sonatype Nexus runs via a Spring Boot launcher; identify by the install prefix in JVM args (LogFile, classpath) since the Nexus main class isn't directly in argv.
Test fixture runs a mock server, not real TeamCity — the actual cmdline is org.mockserver.cli.Main. This rule won't match in the fixture but represents what the real check would target.
comm is exactly 'temporal-server' (15 chars, at the limit).
tls test fixture serves TLS via nginx — same rule as nginx.

results.notes: Embedded tomcat in the test fixture (classpath contains tomcat-embed-core-*.jar); standard tomcat uses the org.apache.catalina.startup.Bootstrap main class.
Apache Traffic Server rewrites argv[0] to [TS_MAIN] / [TS_MANAGER]; comm doesn't match. The real binary appears as the second argv element after argv[0]. Use cmdline.contains on the binary path.
